item.title: State minister rules out cattle imports as Eid surplus expected at 2.2 million
item.description: Sultan Salauddin Tuku told reporters that an estimated 12.3 million cattle are ready across the country.

item.title: H&M reshuffles Bangladeshi supplier base amid shifting global demand
item.description: The Swedish company buys an estimated $3 to 3.5 billion in garments and textiles from Bangladesh annually, making the country one of its largest production hubs.

item.title: Death toll jumps to 90 in China coal mine blast
item.description: The gas explosion occurred late on Friday at the Liushenyu coal mine in Qinyuan county, ​with 247 workers on duty underground, state media Xinhua ​reported earlier in the day.

item.title: No new government mills planned, commerce minister says
item.description: The administration, the minister said, was far more interested in strengthening the private sector than in becoming an entrepreneur itself.

item.title: Dhaka, Delhi divided over water-sharing basis as Ganges treaty nears expiry
item.description: Neither government has yet said whether the existing pact will be renewed or replaced, but their respective ministries confirm that technical teams from both sides have begun preliminary work.

item.title: Three months in, is Trump losing the Iran war?
item.description: The crisis is not yet over, and some experts leave open the possibility Trump might still find a face-saving way out if negotiations break in his favor.

item.title: Gabbard resigns as Trump’s top US intelligence official
item.description: Gabbard ​advised Trump of her intention to step down during an Oval Office meeting on Friday, Fox News Digital reported earlier. The resignation is effective June 30, it said.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold prices for domestic market
item.description: Under the adjustment, 22-carat gold now sells at BDT 235,963 a bhori, down from BDT 238,121.

item.title: Rubio’s trip to India signals US need to repair ties
item.description: The four-day trip, Rubio’s first visit to India, will include stops in Kolkata, Agra, Jaipur and New Delhi for talks ‌focused on trade, energy and defense cooperation, the State Department said.

item.title: Measles and measles-like symptoms claim 11 more lives; 1,104 hospitalised
item.description: Since March 15, in total, 499 children have died from measles and measles-like symptoms across the country so far, according to the DGHS data.

item.title: BSF attempts to install pillars at Patgram Border, BGB prevents move
item.description: When the matter came to the attention of local residents and BGB personnel, members of the Kolsirmukh Border Outpost (BOP) immediately lodged strong objections and took a firm position at the site. Due to firm protests, the BSF was forced to stop its activities.

item.title: Bangladesh emerges as world’s leading sourcing hub for low-cost T-shirts
item.description: Bangladesh overtook China in 2011 to become the world’s leading exporter of cotton T-shirts, according to the report. In 2024, Bangladesh accounted for 20 percent of global cotton T-shirt exports.

item.title: Thomas Dooley appointed new head coach of Bangladesh national football team
item.description: BFF is hopeful and thrilled to appoint American-origin German Thomas Dooley as the new head coach of the Bangladesh national football team.

item.title: Prime Minister visits Ramisa’s family
item.description: The prime minister has also taken responsibility for the education and future support of Ramisa’s elder sister, Raisa Akter.

item.title: SpaceX scrubs launch of upgraded Starship from Texas, to retry Friday
item.description: Starship V3, uncrewed and featuring dozens of upgrades tailored for rapid Starlink satellite launches and NASA moon missions, was to be a key test for the vehicle following months of testing delays.

item.title: Korea Post eyes AI data centres, real estate investment for higher returns amid mail losses
item.description: Korea Post is still cautious on office ​buildings, but is looking at so-called secondary funds investing in data centres, logistics facilities, and multi-family houses in North America and ​Europe.

item.title: India’s job engine strains as Iran war hits remittances and trade
item.description: The foreign conflict has dealt a double blow to the economy, with returning migrant workers stuck in India and unable to find similar ‌pay in their hometowns, heightening the risk of social unrest as unemployment grows.

item.title: Measles vaccination programme to continue regularly under EPI: Health Minister
item.description: The health minister said civil surgeons, directors, and hospital superintendents across the country have been instructed to continue vaccination activities.

item.title: Info minister seeks UK, Swiss cooperation in ensuring media freedom
item.description: The information said that the government is determined to ensure media freedom, and work has already begun to form a Media Commission.

item.title: Samsung’s deal with union hailed as a victory as bonuses less generous than SK Hynix’s
item.description: Samsung workers had been furious about the large gap in bonuses with their peers at smaller rival SK Hynix, which had beaten Samsung to the punch ​in delivering advanced AI chip units to Nvidia, bolstering its fortunes.

item.title: Supreme Leader says enriched uranium must stay in Iran, Iranian sources say
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei’s order could further frustrate U.S. President Donald Trump and complicate talks on ending the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Dhaka plans mandatory stock listings for large firms: Finance Minister
item.description: The minister said all economic and administrative systems would be digitised within one year and that a single-card framework would consolidate citizen services.

item.title: Dhaka metro to run limited services during Eid break
item.description: The operator said it planned to deploy roughly 140 train sets across seven lines.

item.title: Lower, middle-income families in Dhaka turning to madrasahs for affordable education
item.description: The total number of primary education institutions in Dhaka district is 5,250. Of these, 4,299 are private — 81.89 percent. In contrast, there are only 951 government primary schools.

item.title: Health sector reform commission meets health minister
item.description: The commission noted that a robust primary healthcare system would help reduce patient pressure on tertiary hospitals, lower treatment costs, and ease the overall burden on specialised facilities.

item.title: Britain clinches $5 billion Gulf trade deal in shadow of Iran war
item.description: The deal will remove 93 percent of GCC tariffs on British goods, equivalent to the removal of £580 million worth of tariffs by the deal’s tenth year, with two-thirds of the tariffs being removed as soon ​as the deal comes into force.

item.title: Ammonium, phosphate levels in Karnaphuli several times higher than normal
item.description: The critical condition poses a serious threat to aquatic life, fish breeding, and the overall ecological balance of the river.

item.title: Bangladesh revenue gap tops BDT 1 trillion in 10 months, imperilling annual target
item.description: Revenue officials had forecast the April–June quarter would bring the sharpest acceleration in collection, but the board’s own figures show the opposite trend has taken hold.

item.title: Power tariff hike proposal sparks fierce opposition at public hearing
item.description: BPDB sought a 17 to 21 percent wholesale price rise that would push the unit rate up by as much as BDT 1.50. The distribution companies requested retail tariff hikes of 15 to 29 percent for different consumer categories.

item.title: The worst climate future is less likely, but the best one is slipping away, scientists say
item.description: Researchers’ new list of seven plausible carbon pollution scenarios for the future are pushing aside two staples of climate policy: the extremes on either end.

item.title: SpaceX IPO filing lays bare losses and Musk control as it stakes future on AI
item.description: For many, Musk’s record turning Tesla into the most ‌valuable auto company in the world and developing the world’s first fully reusable rocket and largest satellite network is enough to justify investment.

item.title: Tehran reviewing latest US response as Trump suggests he can wait
item.description: Six weeks since a fragile ceasefire came into force, talks to end the war have shown ​little progress, while soaring oil prices have raised concern over inflation and the impact on the global economy.

item.title: Law minister orders seven-day deadline for Pallabi child rape-murder probe
item.description: The body of Ramisa Akhter, a second-grade student, was recovered on Tuesday morning from an apartment in Pallabi’s Section-11 area.

item.title: Bangladesh reaffirms commitment to UN peacekeeping, urges stronger safeguards
item.description: Underscoring the country’s staunch multilateralism, the state minister noted it remains one of the largest contributors to UN missions worldwide.

item.title: BAJUS lifts gold prices as domestic pure gold costs climb
item.description: The BAJUS standing committee on pricing and price monitoring set the new rates at a meeting on Thursday morning

item.title: US charges Raúl Castro with murder as Trump escalates pressure on Cuba
item.description: The charges against Castro and five fighter pilots in the Cuban military stem from a 1996 incident in which Cuban jets shot down planes operated by a group of Cuban exiles.

item.title: Dhaka-Chattogram travel time on road and rail rises to 7–8 hours
item.description: Due to short-sighted planning, lack of maintenance, severe traffic congestion, and manpower shortages, the travel time has increased.

item.title: Primary curriculum reform to develop leadership, civic responsibility: State Minister
item.description: State Minister Bobby Hajjaj said extensive reforms under Prime Minister Tarique Rahman aimed to produce skilled, confident and responsible citizens.

item.title: “Higher allocation alone won’t suffice, budget implementation capacity also needed”
item.description: The healthcare sector continues to suffer from poor governance, weak infrastructure, and shortages of doctors and workers. Even the budget allocations made for the sector cannot be utilised effectively. Moreover, the scope of the health sector itself has not been properly defined, and an integrated public health policy has yet to be developed.

item.title: Three more Bangladeshi RMG factories earn LEED Platinum status
item.description: The additions bring the country’s total to 287 LEED-certified factories, of which 124 hold Platinum status and 144 carry Gold ratings, according to certification data.

item.title: PM lays foundation stone of Disaster Management Research and Training Institute building
item.description: According to the project officials, the institute will be established on nearly eight acres of land in the Dharpara area of Sataish Chourasta.

item.title: SpaceX’s upgraded Starship V3 ready for debut launch ahead of IPO
item.description: The towering spacecraft, ⁠consisting of the upper-stage Starship astronaut vessel stacked atop its Super Heavy booster rocket, was due for launch as early as Thursday, the company said late on Monday.

item.title: Xi and Putin unite in criticism of US on nuclear, security issues
item.description: A statement from Xi and Russian President Vladimir Putin said Trump’s plan for a ground- and space-based missile interceptor system posed a threat to global strategic stability.

item.title: Bangladesh WASH budget shrinks 40%, threatening SDG 6 targets and climate resilience
item.description: The decline threatens to reverse decades of progress towards Sustainable Development Goal 6 and undercut climate adaptation in one of the world’s most vulnerable countries.

item.title: PM urges Ansar-VDP members to perform duties with discipline, professionalism
item.description: The prime minister expressed the hope that, through dedication, discipline, professionalism, and patriotism, the Ansar-VDP would emerge as a technology-driven and humanitarian social force in the future.

item.title: Pubali and Uttara banks emerge as privatisation success stories
item.description: The two privatised banks have sustained their expansion for more than a dozen years, steadily adding capital, deposits, loans and profits. Shareholders have drawn steady dividends as well.

item.title: PM greets Tigers on winning Test series against Pakistan
item.description: The prime minister said Bangladesh’s success in international cricket would continue and this achievement would further inspire the younger generation toward sports.

item.title: Rubio heads to a NATO meeting as European angst over Trump reliability, US troops, Iran grows
item.description: The State Department said Tuesday that Rubio would attend the NATO meeting in Helsingborg on Friday, one of the last senior-level NATO gatherings before alliance leaders meet at a summit in Ankara, Turkey, in July.

item.title: Trustworthy accounts central to restoring confidence, minister warns
item.description: The minister called for an ecosystem of full transparency and told members of the country’s two main accountancy bodies that they bore a professional duty to present accurate financial pictures.

item.title: Khulna hospital fire triggers ICU evacuation; patient dies during transfer
item.description: The blaze began around 5:30 am in a room adjacent to the old ICU on the third storey of the four-storey building.

item.title: Trade slowdown deepens Bangladesh’s revenue shortfall
item.description: More than 65 percent of government revenue flows from indirect taxes, tying receipts directly to business conditions. When producers’ sales shrink and consumers pull back, indirect tax revenue shrinks in lockstep.

item.title: BAJUS lowers gold prices, silver stable
item.description: Under the revised rates, 22-carat gold will sell at BDT 235,963 per bhori (11.664 gram) in the local market.

item.title: Xi and Putin meet to reaffirm China-Russia ties days after Trump’s visit to Beijing
item.description: Xi welcomed Putin with a ceremony at the Great Hall of the People. The two delegations later held bilateral talks, to be followed by a ceremony for signing cooperation agreements.

item.title: Bangladesh and Morocco eye broader cooperation in trade, investment
item.description: Both sides also agreed to hold the next round of Foreign Office Consultations in Dhaka at the earliest opportunity to review ongoing work and identify new areas of mutual interest.

item.title: Tankers exit Hormuz as Trump, Vance talk up Iran deal prospects
item.description: Two Chinese ships, among a handful of supertankers carrying Iraqi crude, exiting the Gulf this month, passed through the narrow strait carrying around 4 million barrels of crude, according to data from LSEG and Kpler.

item.title: Singapore seeks expanded investment, trade partnership with Bangladesh
item.description: The Singaporean envoy highlighted the Bay Terminal project as a major area of interest for Singaporean investors, noting that the estimated investment in the project could range between $800 million and $1 billion.

item.title: Eliminating inequality at every level of society is State’s responsibility: Speaker
item.description: The speaker noted that All-Party Caucus could play a role in reducing inequalities across different sectors of the country and society.

item.title: At least 18 killed as heavy rains drench southern, central China
item.description: China’s weather agency said areas of Jiangxi, Anhui, Hunan, Hubei, Guizhou, Guangxi, Guangdong and Hainan faced a ​high risk of rain-related disasters, including landslides, flash floods and severe urban flooding, and waterlogging.

item.title: Nvidia’s outlook will be a test of its strategy to maintain AI dominance
item.description: After years of near-monopoly in chips ​used to train AI systems, Nvidia is facing competition from tech giants building their own chips to capture ‌demand that is shifting toward processors that run AI systems, respond to queries and carry out tasks in real time.

item.title: ILO cooperation vital for ensuring safe work environment, labour welfare: LGRD Minister
item.description: During the meeting between LGRD Minister and ILO Bangladesh Country Director Max Tunon, discussions focused on improving the living standards of city corporation sanitation workers, enhancing skills, creating employment opportunities, and developing modern waste management systems.

item.title: Responsible business conduct crucial for Bangladesh’s trade competitiveness: Commerce Minister
item.description: The minister said that in the rapidly changing reality of the global trade and investment system, Bangladesh is entering a new stage.

item.title: Iran says peace proposal includes reparations for war damage, US troop withdrawal
item.description: In Tehran’s first comments on ‌the proposal, Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi said Tehran also sought the lifting of sanctions, the release of frozen funds and an end to the U.S. marine blockade on the country, according to IRNA news agency.

item.title: CID seizes BDT 35 billion in assets and funds over 15 months
item.description: According to the CID, assets and funds have been seized, frozen, and confiscated through court orders over the 15 months between January 2025 and March 2026.

item.title: DGHS instructs all government hospitals to set up separate measles wards, cabins
item.description: Administrators must ensure sufficient doctors and healthcare staff are deployed to the isolation units, and specialist physicians are to conduct rounds each morning and afternoon, including on holidays.

item.title: Fire service capacity enhancement underway, says home minister
item.description: The home minister said 20 projects for the construction and reconstruction of fire stations are currently being implemented across the country to enhance emergency response services.

item.title: Media commission, policy to be formed by July-August: Info Minister
item.description: Swapon said it would be possible to remove various legal limitations after the formation of the proposed media commission.

item.title: PM for people-friendly land management to build modern state
item.description: The prime minister said the main objective of his government is to establish a corruption-free, harassment-free, technology-driven, and citizen-friendly land management system that will further accelerate the country’s sustainable development.

item.title: Info minister asks Italy to legally hire more Bangladeshi workers
item.description: The minister also put emphasis on making the relations between the two countries more diverse and stronger.

item.title: Petrobangla to launch international tender next week for offshore exploration
item.description: Energy division sources said the tender notice will appear next Sunday, with promotional packages going on sale after that. Companies will have until November 30 to purchase Petrobangla’s seismic data and submit their bids.

item.title: Lebanon death toll reaches 3,000 in fighting between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: Fighting began on March 2 with the Hezbollah militant group firing at Israel, two days after the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran. Attacks from both sides have not stopped despite a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: Five dead, including two teen suspects, after shooting at San Diego mosque
item.description: San Diego Police Chief ‌Scott Wahl said local law enforcement and the FBI were investigating the attack on the largest mosque in San Diego County as a hate crime.

item.title: Trump says he paused attack on Iran, signals a nuclear deal may be possible
item.description: Under pressure to reach an accord that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump has ​previously expressed hope that a deal was close on ending the war, and similarly threatened heavy strikes on Iran if Tehran does not reach a deal.

item.title: Dhaka, Washington reaffirm bilateral ties during state minister’s visit
item.description: The state minister pointed to deepening ties between the US and Bangladesh in trade, energy, technology and education, while reaffirming Dhaka’s interest in regional stability and constructive engagement with its neighbours.

item.title: No deal, no exit: How US-Iran standoff risks fresh conflict
item.description: A growing concern among policymakers is not whether a deal is near, but how long tensions can persist before a miscalculation by Washington or Tehran triggers renewed conflict.

item.title: PM asks women MPs to prepare for effective role in National Parliament
item.description: The prime minister urged the MPs to develop themselves as competent lawmakers by having a clear understanding of the Rules of Procedure of the National Parliament.

item.title: BERC raises furnace oil price by BDT 18.85 per litre
item.description: The new price has been fixed at BDT 113.54 per litre.

item.title: Zilhajj moon sighted, Eid-ul-Adha in Bangladesh on May 28
item.description: Kazi Shah Mofazzal Hossain Kaikobad made the announcement on Monday evening following a meeting of the National Moon Sighting Committee.

item.title: RAB to be made more pro-people, accountable force: Home Minister
item.description: The minister said the government was working to strengthen public trust in the elite force through greater public engagement, transparency, accountability, and adherence to human rights, the rule of law, and professional ethics.

item.title: Pakistan deploys jet squadron, thousands of troops to Saudi Arabia during Iran war
item.description: The deployment, the full scale of which is reported here for the first time, was confirmed by three security officials and two government sources, all of whom described it as a substantial, combat-capable force intended to support Saudi Arabia’s military if the kingdom comes under further attack.

item.title: Banks, NBFIs to remain open on Saturday ahead of week-long Eid holiday
item.description: In two separate circulars issued on Monday, the central bank said branches would handle normal business on May 23 and May 24 and would then close from May 25 to May 31 for the festival.

item.title: NEC approves BDT 3 trillion ADP for FY27
item.description: The new ADP for the fiscal year 2026-27 has been designed with a reform-oriented and inclusive development vision aimed at ensuring sustainable economic growth, balanced regional development, and administrative modernisation.

item.title: BDT 3 trillion ADP budget set with curbs on wasteful projects: Finance Minister
item.description: The outlay, which exceeds the current year’s, was approved at the first meeting of the National Economic Council chaired by Prime Minister Tarique Rahman since his government took office.

item.title: World Bank approves $350 million additional financing for Bangladesh LNG supply
item.description: The fresh funding, cleared on May 15, will provide payment guarantees to the state-owned oil and gas company Petrobangla through standby letters of credit and short-term credit lines.

item.title: Qatar signals demand for Bangladeshi workers in four sectors
item.description: The state minister Nurul Haque added that Qatar’s attitude was positive regarding creating employment opportunities for Bangladeshis in additional sectors as well.

item.title: Bangladesh mulls student laptop loans after training
item.description: The youth department currently extends loans of up to BDT 500,000 at a 5 percent service charge for entrepreneurship and skill development. The proposed laptop credit would be a new strand of that programme.

item.title: Four former bank MDs jailed, others grow anxious
item.description: Beyond the top executives already in prison in Bangladesh, the ACC has also filed cases against around a dozen more bank managing directors. Investigations and case preparations are also underway against top executives of another 10 to 12 banks.

item.title: Bangladesh, New Zealand eye broader economic cooperation
item.description: Both sides held fruitful discussions on bilateral relations, trade expansion and new avenues of mutual cooperation between Bangladesh and New Zealand.

item.title: CEC dismisses NCP leader’s claims, urges political cooperation
item.description: The CEC called for political cooperation, arguing that credible local government elections — expected to begin by the end of the year — would be impossible without it.

item.title: Salt costs, cash shortages strain Jashore’s hide trade ahead of Eid
item.description: A surge in salt prices, a scarcity of formal credit and a mountain of unpaid tannery bills threaten to depress the rawhide trade ahead of the upcoming Eid.

item.title: Russian attacks kill one, injure more than 30 in Ukraine overnight, officials say
item.description: Drones hit ​residential buildings, a school and a kindergarten in the ⁠major Black Sea export port of Odesa, Serhiy Lysak, the head ​of the local military administration, said on messaging app Telegram.

item.title: Water crisis deepens across capital’s Mirpur neighbourhoods, surrounding areas
item.description: Residents say the crisis has dragged on for months. Supply is erratic: in many areas water arrives just once a day, and only briefly; in others it disappears for two or three days at a stretch.

item.title: Content creator Kaarina Kaisar laid to rest in family graveyard
item.description: A fourth and final funeral prayer took place at 8.30 am on Monday in the village of Abdullahpur, in Gajaria upazila, Munshiganj. She was then interred in the family burial ground at her maternal ancestral home.

item.title: PM chairs National Economic Council meeting
item.description: The meeting’s agenda includes a discussion on the Annual Development Programme (ADP) for the 2026–27 fiscal year.

item.title: China agrees to boost trade for US ag products such as beef and poultry following Trump-Xi summit
item.description: China would restore market access for US beef and resume imports of poultry from US states determined by the US Department of Agriculture to be free of the bird flu, the White House said.

item.title: Buet panel urges Dhaka BRT project be scrapped; cabinet to decide
item.description: Back in 2012, the then-government launched the project to connect the Dhaka airport with Gazipur. It has since absorbed BDT 42.5 billion and 97 percent of the work is complete. Experts now urge the project to be scrapped, citing design complications.

item.title: Taiwan’s president defends US arms purchases that Trump called ‘bargaining chip’
item.description: U.S. arms sales to Taiwan and security cooperation between the two sides are not only governed by law but also a catalyst for regional peace and stability, President Lai Ching-te said in a statement.

item.title: CDC aiding withdrawal of affected Americans after Ebola outbreak in Congo, Uganda
item.description: The World ​Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak a public health emergency of ​international concern after 80 suspected deaths.

item.title: Oil touches 2-week high after drone attack on UAE nuclear power plant
item.description: Brent crude futures climbed $2.03, or 1.86 percent, ​to $111.29 a barrel by 0220 GMT, after touching $112 earlier, the highest since May 5.

item.title: UAE and Saudi Arabia report drone incidents as Iran war deadlock drags on
item.description: While hostilities during the Iran conflict have largely been scaled down since a ceasefire came into effect ⁠in April, drones have been launched from Iraq towards Gulf countries, including Saudi Arabia and Kuwait.

item.title: Govt to break oligarchic grip on energy sector: PM Finance Adviser
item.description: PM Finance Adviser criticised the previous government’s energy policies, saying they had created a structurally flawed system benefiting a small group of influential interests.

item.title: Health minister for public awareness to combat measles, dengue
item.description: Sakhawat Hossain said that if breastfeeding, nutritious food, cleanliness, isolation, and timely vaccination can be ensured, both measles and dengue can be largely brought under control.

item.title: Government working to prevent road accidents: Minister
item.description: The minister said that public unawareness and the operation of different types of small and large vehicles on the same roads are also among the major causes of accidents.

item.title: Govt encouraging expansion of export capacity, says state minister
item.description: The state minister said the government is prioritising earning foreign currency through the export of domestically produced goods and farm-based products, while ensuring local demand is met.

item.title: Speaker, French envoy discuss bilateral ties, trade cooperation
item.description: During the meeting, both sides exchanged views on strengthening people-to-people contacts, enhancing inter-parliamentary relations, women’s political empowerment, human rights and the role of Bangladeshi peacekeepers deployed in United Nations peacekeeping missions.

item.title: PM meets Editors’ Council leaders at secretariat
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman, alongside senior ministers and advisers, met Editors’ Council leaders at the secretariat before joining them for a luncheon.

item.title: Ukraine drones kill at least four in Russia, Moscow faces biggest attack in over a year
item.description: Ukraine has stepped up drone attacks on targets deep inside Russia in recent weeks, aiming to knock out oil refineries, depots and pipelines, as both sides seek to degrade each other’s infrastructure.

item.title: Additional Secretary Abdul Mannan appointed as Petrobangla chairman
item.description: Md Abdul Mannan, who serves in the planning sub-division of the Energy and Mineral Resources Division, was handed the additional charge of chairman on Sunday by a routine office order.

item.title: Student clash over DUET vice-chancellor leaves 20 injured
item.description: Tensions flared when the protesting group padlocked the main gate and the pro-appointment side attempted to force it open.

item.title: Hajj 2026: 60,991 Bangladeshi pilgrims reach Saudi Arabia so far
item.description: A total of 78,500 people from Bangladesh have been allowed to perform Hajj this year.

item.title: Minister says roadmap set for leather sector’s short- and long-term development
item.description: Preservation capacity must be expanded, the industries minister said, to prevent losses and turn the sector into a significant income stream.

item.title: Afsana Begum reappointed as director of National Book Centre
item.description: In September 2025, the interim government appointed Afsana Begum as director of the National Book Centre for a two-year term. However, she was relieved of the position in January, before the completion of her tenure.

item.title: PM’s ICT adviser hopes Bangladesh joins top 20 digital economies within five years
item.description: Rehan Asif Asad, speaking at a Dhaka discussion marking World Telecommunication and Information Society Day, noted that the ambition was rooted in long-term international data.

item.title: PM attends funeral prayer of ex-State Minister Mizanur Rahman Sinha
item.description: After the Janaza, Prime Minister Tarique Rahman paid tribute to Mizanur Rahman Sinha by placing a wreath on the coffin of the deceased.

item.title: Mahasthangarh: an archaeological site on the verge of destruction; artefacts being stolen
item.description: The heritage is gradually fading due to neglect and poor maintenance. Many important structures are slowly disappearing due to a lack of regular preservation efforts. Waterlogging during the monsoon, the spread of weeds, and soil erosion have collectively placed the existence of the archaeological site under serious threat.

item.title: Government borrows BDT 460 billion through treasury bills in April
item.description: Bangladesh Bank data show gross treasury bill issuance totalled BDT 460 billion in April 2026. Of that, BDT 328 billion went to repaying maturing obligations, leaving net issuance at BDT 132 billion.

item.title: Putin to visit Chinese leader Xi Jinping days after Trump’s trip to Beijing
item.description: The announcement comes less than 24 hours after U.S. President Donald Trump finished his own state visit to China, where he also met Xi to discuss trade and the U.S. and Israel’s war in Iran.

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hamas’ military wing leader, who Israel says was an architect of Oct 7 attacks
item.description: Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed on Friday, Israel’s army said, describing him as one of the senior Hamas military commanders who directed the planning and execution of the Hamas-led attacks on October 7, 2023.

item.title: Trump returns from China with stability and a stalemate
item.description: Two days of talks between Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping underscored that even after Trump’s “Liberation Day” tariffs and the ensuing trade detente the ‌two sides reached late last year, Washington and Beijing are still locked in the contest that Trump inherited when he started his second term.

item.title: Moon sighting committee meets Monday to fix Eid-ul-Adha date
item.description: The committee will assemble at 6:45 pm local time tomorrow in the meeting room of the Islamic Foundation at Baitul Mukarram.

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Congo, Uganda an emergency of international concern
item.description: The WHO said the outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo virus, does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency but that countries sharing land borders with DRC ​are at high risk for further spread.

item.title: Canada deepens Arctic defense ties with Nordics after Trump threats
item.description: As Canada attempts to pivot away from relying on the US to protect its vast Arctic, Prime Minister ​Mark Carney is strengthening ties and exchanging security tips with the Nordic countries, which he describes as trusted partners.

item.title: Trump says Xi agrees Iran must open strait, China says war should not have started
item.description: Flying back from Beijing on Friday after two days of talks with Xi, Trump said he was considering whether to lift U.S. sanctions on Chinese oil companies buying Iranian oil. China is the biggest buyer of Iranian oil.

item.title: Trump’s geopolitical brinkmanship has hit a wall with Iran
item.description: With the two sides deadlocked, Trump has signaled growing frustration over the 11-week-old crisis ​but has shown little inclination to soften his harsh diplomatic approach toward Iran’s leaders.

item.title: Farakka agreement signing must maintain Bangladesh’s interests, says LGRD minister
item.description: The minister said Bangladesh would not permit any agreement that compromised its interests.

item.title: BAU innovation sorts 500kg of potatoes an hour at minimal cost
item.description: The new device sorts more than 500 kg of potatoes per hour at a cost of just 12 poisha (BDT 0.12) per kilogram, making it roughly 70 times cheaper than manual grading, according to the research team’s calculations.

item.title: Govt to distribute 9,800 tonnes of salt for preserving sacrificial animal hides
item.description: The Bangladesh Small and Cottage Industries Corporation (BSCIC) will complete the initiative within the next week.

item.title: PM inaugurates Khordda Canal re-excavation in Chandpur
item.description: The prime minister arrived at the site by road around 2.40 pm and unveiled a plaque, then cut the soil with a spade to launch the work and planted saplings on the canal bank.

item.title: Island district Bhola’s enduring role in national politics over six decades
item.description: After Bangladesh’s independence, the district remained a deeply active and consequential force in national politics. Several national-level figures have risen from Bhola, and they have helped chart the country’s political course — from both inside and outside the corridors of power.

item.title: JU academic exhibition challenges Johari Window theory
item.description: Associate Professor Salma Ahmed and students of the department’s 53rd batch jointly organised the event as part of the JMS 202: Interpersonal and Group Communication course.

item.title: Bangladesh, Angola launch energy partnership talks
item.description: The landmark virtual meeting, held at Bangladesh’s initiative on Friday, brought together senior government officials and state energy entities from both countries.

item.title: Hajj 2026: 59,055 Bangladeshi pilgrims reach Saudi Arabia so far
item.description: A total of 660 agencies are handling Hajj operations under private management, including 30 lead agencies and 630 coordinating agencies.

item.title: Bangladesh, Brazil eye strategic partnership, deeper cooperation
item.description: Both sides underscored the need to transform the existing friendly relations into a strategic partnership, reflecting the growing convergence between Bangladesh and Brazil as emerging voices of the Global South.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold, silver prices
item.description: Under the revised rates, 22-carat gold will sell at BDT 238,121 per bhori (11.664 gram) in the local market.

item.title: Power Grid Bangladesh sinks deeper into loss as debt nears BDT 600 billion
item.description: Senior executives say transmission volumes have undershot targets, creating a revenue shortfall that feeds directly into the losses. The utility must shoulder heavy operating costs even though much of its newly built infrastructure sits underused.

item.title: US government planning dramatic Colorado River water cuts due to drought, overuse
item.description: The maximum possible federal cuts, ​which would be reviewed every two years based on water levels, are nearly twice as large as ​a May 1 offer by those three lower-basin states to reduce their water use by 1.6 million acre-feet per year.

item.title: SpaceX accelerates IPO timeline, targets June 12 listing on Nasdaq, sources say
item.description: SpaceX’s debut comes at ​a pivotal moment for the IPO market, which has rebounded after struggling over the past couple of years amid volatility ​fueled by U.S. tariff policy and geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: US plans to indict Raul Castro raise Cubans’ fears of force
item.description: An indictment of the 94-year-old revolutionary icon would mark a major escalation in the pressure campaign against Cuba by the Trump administration.

item.title: Pageantry over policy: Takeaways from Trump’s China trip
item.description: Trump ended his trip without any obvious sign of securing China’s help, and it remains unclear how hard China is willing to push for Iran to do a deal or what it would want in return for its help.

item.title: Mizanur Rahman Sinha, former state minister, dies at 83
item.description: Mizanur Rahman Sinha served as state minister for health and family welfare from 2003 to 2006 and won the Munshiganj-2 parliamentary seat on the BNP nomination in the 1996 and 2001 general elections.

item.title: Iran has ‘no trust’ in US, will negotiate only if it is serious, Araqchi says
item.description: Araqchi told reporters in New Delhi that all vessels can pass through the Strait of ​Hormuz except those “at war” with Tehran, if they coordinate with Iran’s navy.

item.title: Trump leaves Beijing with few wins but warm words for Xi
item.description: Trump’s visit to America’s ​main strategic and economic rival, the first by a U.S. president since his last trip in 2017, aimed for tangible results to beef up his sagging approval ratings before the midterm elections.

item.title: Canal excavation programme will bring positive changes to rural economy: Minister
item.description: The minister said that using canal water would increase irrigation facilities, drainage systems would help eliminate waterlogging, and opportunities would be created for tree plantation and fish farming along canal banks.

item.title: Sports key to keeping children away from drugs: Energy Minister
item.description: The minister urged ensuring at least two hours of daily sports activities for children and adolescents to help them grow up in a healthy and sound environment.

item.title: Bangladesh’s foreign currency reserve stands at $34.32 billion
item.description: Officials noted that the reserve position reflects the country’s external sector stability amidst ongoing global economic uncertainties.

item.title: Bangladesh, US seal landmark energy cooperation pact
item.description: The MoU would support Bangladesh’s efforts to strengthen long-term energy security by diversifying its energy sourcing options based on affordability and supply chain sustainability.

item.title: Measles and measles-like symptoms claim 12 more children
item.description: DGHS Health Emergency Operation Center & Control Room disclosed the information on Friday afternoon.

item.title: Padma River’s course will change further over the next decade
item.description: Researchers stated that climate change and Padma Bridge’s impact will play major roles in this transformation, adding that the rapidly changing course of the river could significantly affect Bangladesh’s geography, public life, and economy.

item.title: Iran and UAE clash at BRICS foreign ministers’ meeting
item.description: ⁠The Iranian foreign minister stated that the truth is that the UAE was directly involved in the aggression ​against his country, adding that when the attacks started, they didn’t even issue a condemnation.

item.title: Boeing shares drop 4% after Trump announces China orders just 200 jets
item.description: A large Boeing order was one of many ⁠business deals expected to come out of the closely watched summit, as well as an extension of a fragile trade truce struck last October, ​when Trump suspended triple-digit tariffs on Chinese goods, and Xi backed away from choking global supplies of vital rare earths.

item.title: Govt to introduce special welfare card for cultural activists: Cultural Minister
item.description: The cultural minister said the government is taking effective measures to preserve the country’s archaeological sites, folk heritage, and historical landmarks, adding that protecting heritage means safeguarding the roots of the nation.

item.title: Shama Obaed Islam reaffirms peacekeeping, multilateral commitments at UN
item.description: During the meeting, the state minister outlined the key priorities of Bangladesh’s newly elected government, emphasising the restoration of public trust, strengthening democratic institutions, enhancing transparency and accountability, improving public service delivery, and upholding human rights and the rule of law.

item.title: Gold prices reduced, per bhori now at BDT 242,000
item.description: The new prices came into effect at 10 am on the same day.

item.title: FM joins UNGA presidency dialogue process
item.description: President of the 80th session of the General Assembly Annalena Baerbock convened the informal interactive dialogues with candidates for the presidency of the 81st UN session on Wednesday, which was broadcast live on UN WebTV.

item.title: Ensure proper use of public money: LGRD Minister
item.description: The minister said public welfare is the main objective of the government’s development activities, and all concerned must attach the highest importance to ensuring that not a single penny of public money is wasted.

item.title: Government issues notification declaring 7-day holiday for Eid-ul-Adha
item.description: All offices will remain closed for seven consecutive days from May 25 to May 31 for Eid-ul-Adha.

item.title: Majority of measles deaths among infants under 9 months
item.description: Despite the launch of special vaccination campaigns, the situation has not improved significantly. The concern is that infants below nine months of age, those still too young to receive the vaccine, are accounting for the highest number of deaths.

item.title: EC publishes updated electoral roll with over 128 million voters
item.description: The commission attributed the 0.5 percent increase to the enrolment of applicants born on or before January 1, 2008.

item.title: Labour minister orders all factories to pay Eid bonuses by May 21
item.description: The minister said local administrations, district police and industrial police had been placed on special alert ahead of the Eid period.

item.title: ‘Actuary’ in insurance industry and its importance, current status in Bangladesh
item.description: Actuarial science is a highly respected profession and offers both prestige and substantial financial rewards. Unfortunately, in Bangladesh, the actuarial profession has not yet developed adequately.

item.title: SSC exams to begin on January 7, HSC on June 6: Edu Minister
item.description: Education Minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon announced the dates on Thursday at a press conference.

item.title: AI boom puts SK Hynix on cusp of $1 trillion market value
item.description: Valued at less than $100 ​billion just 16 months ago, SK Hynix’s market capitalisation stood at roughly $942 billion at the ⁠close of trading on Thursday.

item.title: EU ambassador calls on opposition leader
item.description: Their discussions focused on making democracy more meaningful after the national parliamentary election, ongoing reform initiatives and the implementation of referendum verdicts.

item.title: Govt plans land and tax incentives for private solar investment, minister says
item.description: The government wanted solar investment to match the scale of the country’s garments industry, the power minister said, arguing that upfront concessions would later generate revenue for the state.

item.title: Repayment pressure, no alternative jobs leave many farmers considering migration
item.description: For the farming communities of the haor region, migration has become a last refuge — a desperate move toward the cities in search of survival. But this path is not open to everyone.

item.title: Janata Bank’s capital shortfall hits BDT 644 billion as bad loans surge
item.description: Virtually all of Janata Bank’s large loans have soured, and it lacks adequate collateral against them. Attempts to sell pledged assets have also drawn no buyers. That has put two-thirds of its loan book at severe risk.

item.title: UAE denies Netanyahu secretly visited the country during the Iran war
item.description: The UAE’s official WAM news agency later posted an article denying “reports circulating” about a Netanyahu visit.

item.title: Russia pounds Ukraine in massive air attack
item.description: Kyiv-area officials reported ​one person killed and several dozen wounded in the ⁠overnight strike, which Prime Minister Yuliia Svyrydenko said targeted civilian infrastructure and ​residential buildings.

item.title: Bangladesh bets on Padma Barrage to revive rivers, boost farming
item.description: Officials estimate the barrage will store about 2,900 million cubic metres of water in the Padma, sustaining flow in roughly 623 small and large rivers across the region during the dry months.

item.title: Fitch cuts Bangladesh outlook to ‘negative’
item.description: The ratings agency ​warned Bangladesh faced significant downside risks through higher energy import ​costs and potential ​disruptions to remittances.

item.title: Indian rupee hits record low as worries over energy risks deepen
item.description: The ​rupee has declined 1.4 percent this week and ‌hit ⁠record lows in each trading session between Tuesday and Thursday.

item.title: UK’s Starmer faces biggest challenge yet as resignation threat overshadows King’s Speech
item.description: As Starmer and his ministers stood in silence to hear King Charles read out that agenda, the ceremony was overshadowed by what could be the most dangerous threat to the prime minister.

item.title: Protests flare across Havana as power cuts deepen amid US blockade
item.description: Cuba’s energy and mines minister earlier ​in the day said the nation had completely run ​out of diesel and fuel oil, and that its power grid had entered ‌a “critical” ⁠state.

item.description: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran looms large over Trump’s visit ‌to China, the first by a U.S. president since his last trip there in 2017.

item.description: Xi opened the summit by telling Trump that stable China–U.S. relationship benefits the entire world.

item.description: Trump is seeking to snag some economic wins on the ‌first visit by a U.S. president to China in nearly a decade and maintain a fragile trade truce to prop up public approval ratings bruised by his war with Iran.

item.title: PM directs authorities to ensure rights, services for persons with disabilities
item.description: The prime minister emphasised the importance of establishing disability-friendly infrastructure and ensuring access to all forms of facilities, including education and healthcare, to build an inclusive society.

item.description: The state minister stated that the importance of livestock and biodiversity is immense in maintaining environmental balance and ensuring food security for the people.

item.description: The Foundation donated the vaccine to make the ongoing activities of the Bangladesh government’s Expanded Programme on Immunisation (EPI) more rapid, dynamic, and effective.

item.title: Govt raises rawhide prices ahead of Eid-ul-Adha
item.description: The rate marks a modest increase from BDT 60–65 last year. Outside the capital, cow hides were fixed at BDT 57–62, up from the previous year’s BDT 55–60.

item.title: Measles and related symptoms claim eight more children in 24 hours
item.description: Confirmed measles claimed one more life in the last 24 hours, bringing the total number of confirmed measles-related deaths to 69 during the same period.

item.description: A patrol from the Shelarchar outpost and a specialist smart team detected the bandits, who opened fire first. The forest guards returned seven rounds.

item.description: Among the approved projects, three are new, five are revised, and one project got an extension of tenure without witnessing cost escalation.

item.title: Exploring opportunities and challenges to improving Bangladesh–Pakistan relationship
item.description: The Pakistan-Bangladesh relationship in 2026 is no longer defined by the past, but by pragmatic necessity.

item.description: Industrial Police said they are maintaining regular communication with factory owners and trying to resolve the situation.

item.title: Import-heavy gas policy proved self-defeating
item.description: The country began importing LNG in 2018 to ease a chronic domestic supply crunch. Seven years on, the import bill has more than quintupled. Energy experts and economists now regard the gas sector’s master plan as a self-defeating decision.

item.description: In an August 2024 lawsuit, Musk accused Altman and OpenAI of persuading him into giving $38 million, only to see the ‌nonprofit abandon its mission to benefit humanity and instead become a for-profit corporation.

item.description: The repayment trajectory has been consistently upward over the five years, indicating mounting pressure from foreign liabilities and the repayment schedule.

item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said high costs and bureaucracy were hurting business, putting jobs and the prosperity of future generations ⁠at risk.

item.description: The minister acknowledged persistent strains across the financial system but insisted recovery was underway. “It may be taking some time. But we will recover,” he said.

item.description: Noting that research publications, citations and innovation are given significant importance in global rankings, the premier said educationists must think more deeply about where Bangladesh stand in these areas.

item.description: Successive governments shied away from authorising extraction at four major coal fields. Import dependence consequently deepened, leaving the vast resource stranded underground.

item.description: The prime minister also flagged a growing tendency for graduates to remain unemployed and said the government was updating syllabi to close the gap.

item.description: The group had been aboard the MV Hondius, a luxury ​expedition cruise ship linked to an outbreak of the Andes virus, the only hantavirus species known to be capable ​of limited spread from person-to-person.

item.description: The U.S. Treasury move follows sanctions announced on Friday on individuals and companies aiding Iranian purchases of weapons and components used to make drones and ballistic missiles.

item.description: Netanyahu said Israel ​receives about $3.8 billion of U.S. military aid a year. The U.S. has agreed to provide a total of $38 billion ​in military aid to Israel from 2018 to 2028.

item.description: The 76-year-old tycoon remade and dominated Thai politics for a quarter-century, but his influence has waned of late.

item.description: Hantaviruses ⁠are a group of viruses that are usually spread by rodents but ​in rare cases can be transmitted person to person.

item.description: Trump is scheduled to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday, ahead of talks set to take place Thursday and Friday. It will be his first trip to China since 2017.
